data.title: Overvalued? Analysts Say These 3 High P/E Stocks Could Jump 60%+
data.description:
High P/E stocks often divide investors. While some view them as overvalued, others see them as companies with strong long-term growth potential. Using the TipRanks Stock Screener Tool, we identified three companies with high price-to-earnings (P/E) ratios that carry Strong Buy consensus ratings and offer more than 60% upside over the next 12 months.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

Double your APP exposure with Tradr's APPX

Why Invest in High P/E Stocks?

An investment's true value depends on its future growth potential. While a high P/E ratio may seem expensive, it often reflects expectations of strong earnings growth. The key is whether that growth justifies the current valuation. Companies with high P/E ratios often have strong fundamentals and significant long-term growth opportunities.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Dow Jones Gains as Oil Slips Below Key $100 Level
data.description:
The Dow Jones (DJIA) opened Friday on higher ground alongside a drop in oil prices.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

After surging above $100 per barrel on Thursday, Brent crude (BZ), the global oil benchmark, is back below the key level. That has also helped ease long-term Treasury yields, which rose earlier this week as higher oil prices fueled concerns about rising inflation. At the same time, tensions between the U.S. and Iran remain elevated.

U.S. Eyes Major Iran Attack as Retaliation Threats Grow

President Trump told Axios on Thursday that he is considering a "massive attack" against Iran. Furthermore, his patience for a diplomatic solution with Iran is quickly fading, sources told the Wall Street Journal.

Meanwhile, Iran is ready to respond and expand the war if Trump follows through with the threat. Earlier this week, Tehran called on the Houthi military group to enact a maritime blockade on Saudi vessels in the Bab al-Mandeb Strait. Iran is prepared to fully close the waterway and directly strike Tel Aviv if the U.S. proceeds with a major offensive campaign, two Iranian officials told the New York Post.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Futures Rise as Oil Prices Ease; Intel Stock Up on Solid Revenue Growth
data.description:
U.S. stock futures edged higher on Friday morning as oil prices trended lower. Investors also cheered Intel's (INTC) second-quarter results, with the chipmaker reporting its highest quarterly revenue growth in about 15 years. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 Index (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were up 0.22%, 0.28%, and 0.40%, respectively, at 8:35 a.m. EDT on July 24.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

At the time of writing, Brent crude (CM:BZ) was down 3.09% to $97.61 per barrel, while WTI crude (CM:CL) slipped 2.86% to $89.66 per barrel. The decline came despite U.S. President Donald Trump saying that he will soon make a decision on whether to launch a "massive attack" on Iran (according to Axios) after the conflict in the Middle East spread to the Red Sea.

On Thursday, major U.S. stock indices fell sharply as investors worried about rising AI spending following earnings reports from Tesla (TSLA) and Alphabet (GOOGL). The Nasdaq Composite dropped 2.15%, while the S&P 500 and Dow Jones fell 1.21% and 0.97%, respectively.

In Friday's pre-market trading, Oracle (ORCL) stock was up more than 2% after the company announced a $7 billion software agreement with the Pentagon. Also, Amkor Technology (AMKR) stock surged 13% as the semiconductor packaging company entered into a $1.5 billion multi-year deal with Nvidia (NVDA).
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: SpaceX Q2 Earnings Are Coming. Let's Look at Who Owns SPCX Stock
data.description:
SpaceX (SPCX) stock trended lower in Friday's pre-market trading and has lost nearly half its value from its post-IPO peak. Investors are now looking ahead to the company's Q2 earnings report on August 4.  Meanwhile, on Thursday, SpaceX delayed its Starship Flight 13 launch to July 24 due to bad weather. The delay follows an earlier launch attempt that was called off just before liftoff. With SpaceX back in focus, we take a closer look at who owns SPCX stock.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

High conviction on SPCX? Try Tradr's SPCM or SPCG

For context, SpaceX is best known for its reusable rockets and Starlink satellite internet service. The company also has growing plans in AI, including data centers in space.

The Q2 report will give investors their first detailed look at SpaceX's results since its IPO. The report will also trigger an early end to part of the company's post-IPO lock-up period. As a result, up to 911.5 million shares held by insiders could become eligible for sale just two trading days after the earnings report.

Who Owns SpaceX Stock?

According to TipRanks' Ownership Tool, public companies and individual investors own 98.15% of SPCX stock. ETFs hold another 1.82%, while other institutional investors own just 0.04%.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Coinbase Stock Still Holds Strong Upside Potential Whether or Not the CLARITY Act Wins
data.description:
U.S. lawmakers are pretty close to a vote on the CLARITY Act. This bill would set clearer legal rules for digital coins. It aims to move crypto oversight away from strict regulators and give legal backing to the systems American crypto firms use.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

While Coinbase stock (COIN) dropped nearly 3% yesterday to $160.33, COIN shares are still up 9.7% this month. The company holds strong upside, according to analysts, even if Congress fails to pass the bill before its August break. Moreover, deeper Wall Street adoption and friendlier rule changes could offer an easier path forward for Coinbase.

Political Fights Are Stalling The CLARITY Act

Passing the bill in the Senate takes at least 60 votes. This means that Republicans need help from seven Democrats. Progress stopped over ethics rules linked to Donald Trump's money disclosures. Public records showed Trump made over $1 billion from crypto deals last year. Due to this, Democrats want a full ban on government officials running crypto businesses. Republicans offered a smaller ban run by the Justice Department, but Democrats turned it down.

Time is running out before the Senate goes on break on August 7. Missing this date could freeze crypto laws for years. This wait does increase short-term fear for Coinbase stockholders, mainly because smaller coins made up half of its first-quarter trading sales.

Furthermore, Coinbase Chief Executive Officer Brian Armstrong backed the bill in a web video, saying the law "cements consumer protections and America's lead in crypto innovation."

Big Banks Are Advancing Blockchain Tools

Washington policy experts say current fights focus on politician ethics, and not on whether crypto should exist. Chris Niebuhr, an analyst at Beacon Policy Advisors, said, "There's been an acceptance that crypto exists as a legitimate product," adding that "I don't think at this point Democrats can or will return to that old crackdown style."

In addition, larger financial firms are moving ahead with blockchain tools on their own. Financial processor Depository Trust & Clearing Corp. tested a token plan with top banks in July and aims for an October start.

Plus, federal watchdogs, under the Trump plan, want to have clearer rules that let firms test digital assets without waiting for Congress. At the same time, the crypto group Fairshake had almost $126 million in cash at the end of May to help pro-crypto politicians.

Some Analysts Think Coinbase Is a Sell

Wider use of digital coins brings new risk for Coinbase. As top banks build their own crypto platforms, Coinbase could face lower trading fees and lose customers.

Compass Point Research & Trading analyst Ed Engel holds a Sell rating on Coinbase stock due to new rivals. But Engel said that "for the industry as a whole, if the Clarity Act doesn't pass, there are enough important things that could happen in the second half of the year to get people excited about the space," adding that "for at least the next two to three years, blockchain has the opportunity to prove it can actually create some solutions."

Is Coinbase a Good Stock to Buy Now?

According to TipRanks, Coinbase stock (COIN) has a consensus Moderate Buy rating among 27 Wall Street analysts. This rating is based on 20 Buy, five Hold, and two Sell ratings assigned in the past three months. The average 12-month COIN price target of $223.04 implies 38.40% upside from current levels. (See COIN stock forecast)
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Gorman-Rupp (GRC) Stock Rallies Despite Mixed Earnings and a CFO Change
data.description:
Gorman-Rupp (GRC) stock rallied on Friday following the release of the pump manufacturer's Q2 2026 earnings report and other updates. First off, the company posted diluted earnings per share of 74 cents alongside revenue of $186.1 million. Investors will note that the company's EPS surpassed Wall Street's estimate of 70 cents per share, while revenue missed the $188.89 million estimate.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Even with the mixed Q2 results, Gorman-Rupp announced a cash dividend to go with its latest earnings. The company will pay a 19-cent per share dividend on September 10, 2026 to shareholders who are on record as of August 14, 2026. The company highlighted that this will be its 306th consecutive quarterly dividend.

Gorman-Rupp Announces a Leadership Change

In addition to the earnings and dividend news, Gorman-Rupp also noted that it has started a succession plan for its Chief Financial Officer. James Kerr, the current CFO and EVP of the company, will be succeeded by Vice President of Finance Ronald Stoops. This will happen on October 1, 2026, though Kerr will remain with the company in an advisory role until his retirement at a later date.

Scott King, president and CEO of Gorman-Rupp, said, "We wish Jim a happy retirement and are excited that Ron will continue Jim's work to help further capitalize on the opportunities that are in front of us."

What's Next for Gorman-Rupp Stock?

While Gorman-Rupp didn't provide formal guidance in its latest earnings report, investors can look to its backlog to get an idea of what's next for the company. It had a backlog of $239.7 million as of June 30, 2026, compared to its backlog of $224.4 million one year ago. Investors will also note that the company saw incoming orders of $370.8 million for the first half of 2026.

Is Gorman-Rupp Stock a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

Turning to Wall Street, the analysts' consensus rating for Gorman-Rupp is Hold, based on one Hold rating over the past three months. Four-star Freedom Capital Markets analyst Sergey Glinyanov is behind this Hold rating. He also has a $74 price target for GRC stock, which represents a potential 7.06% drop.

Glinyanov will likely update his rating and price target for Gorman-Rupp stock after today's earnings report. It's also possible that other analysts will weigh in on the company. (See GRC Stock Forecast)
data.author: William White

data.title: SpaceX AI Push Is Getting Bigger — Here's What Wedbush Says
data.description:
SpaceX (SPCX) is reportedly looking at building data centers in Texas as Elon Musk grows the company's AI business. Wedbush analyst Matt Bryson said the report is in line with the firm's industry checks. Those checks show strong demand from SpaceX for parts needed for its data center plans from 2027 onward.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

Explore NVDS for 2X short leverage on NVDA

The news suggests that SpaceX plans to expand its AI data center network. The company already has large data centers in Memphis and is adding more. New sites in Texas could help SpaceX add even more computing power.

SpaceX Expands Its AI Data Center Footprint

SpaceX's AI data center network has grown quickly. Colossus 1 in Memphis opened in 2024, while work on Colossus 2 began in 2025. The company has also started work on a third data center in Memphis.

Texas could now become another site for this growth. The plans also point to strong demand for AI computing in the years ahead.

The buildout could also benefit Nvidia (NVDA). Large AI data centers need many advanced chips and servers. Bryson believes strong demand for AI computing should support Nvidia as firms such as SpaceX spend more on these systems.

SpaceX Q2 Earnings Are Coming Up

Investors could learn more about SpaceX's AI plans when the company reports Q2 results on August 4. Wall Street expects revenue of about $6.93 billion and a loss of $0.22 per share. In Q1, SpaceX reported nearly $4.7 billion in revenue and a loss of $1.27 per share.

This will be SpaceX's first quarterly report as a listed company. Investors will look for more details on its spending plans. Any update from Musk could also shed more light on how fast the AI business may grow.

What Is SPCX's Stock Price Target? 

SPCX stock has a Moderate Buy consensus rating on TipRanks based on 23 Buys, six Holds, and one Sell assigned after its IPO. The average price target for SpaceX is $239.04, implying an upside of 102.16% from the current price.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: AMD Stock Forecast: Why This Five-Star Analyst Raised the Chipmaker's Price Target by 41%
data.description:
Shares in Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) edged higher during Friday's pre-market session. This came after Benchmark's five-star analyst Cody Acree raised his price target on the U.S. chipmaker by 41% to reflect its recent deals with tech giant Microsoft (MSFT) and AI startup Anthropic.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

AMD Secures New Microsoft, Anthropic Deals

On Monday, AMD announced that Microsoft (MSFT) will use its latest GPUs, CPUs, networking technology, and software to boost its Azure cloud platform. This capacity will be offered through AMD's Helios rack-scale AI system.

The chipmaker officially launched the system on Thursday at its flagship Advanced AI 2026 event. The system combines AMD's latest Instinct MI455X GPUs and the EPYC "Venice" CPUs. It also runs on the chipmaker's Pensando Vulcano network interface cards and ROCm open software for running AI workloads. 

Similarly, AMD this week announced that Anthropic will also use Helios to generate up to 2GW of cloud computing capacity. In return, the chipmaker will use the startup's Claude AI model to improve the performance of its Instinct GPUs and further develop the ROCm software.

Why Is Benchmark Bullish on AMD's New Deals?

Reacting to the deals, Acree on Thursday maintained his Buy rating on AMD stock and lifted his price target from $485 to $685. The new price target implies about 27% upside from Thursday's closing price of $539.69.

The analyst believes that Microsoft's adoption of Helios in Azure gives AMD an earlier, more immediate chance to prove how powerful the system is. He also sees software development benefits from the Anthropic deal.

Acree argued that both deals offer more benefits to AMD than when considered separately.

Who Is Cody Acree?

As a five-star analyst, Acree ranks 38th out of the more than 12,000 Wall Street analysts tracked on TipRanks. He focuses on the U.S. tech industry and also covers companies such as Nvidia (NVDA), Intel (INTC), and Qualcomm (QCOM).

Acree currently boasts a 69% success rate and has generated about a 36% average return for investors with his ratings.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: SAP Stock Soars after the Software Giant's Cloud Deals Silence AI Replacement Fears
data.description:
Scared investors have spent months selling off SAP stock (SAP) because they've been worried that new artificial intelligence tools would replace SAP's older business software. This fear caused a huge drop in, pulling SAP stock down 40% earlier this year. SAP has just proved these worries wrong. The German software firm posted second-quarter earnings that beat Wall Street targets, sending SAP stock soaring.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

SAP's saw impressive sales of its cloud storage products, and this showed that major companies still need SAP to run their daily business.

SAP's Cloud Sales Have Created a Strong Revenue Beat

High demand for SAP's web systems helped lift total sales by 11% to €9.88 billion ($11.24 billion). SAP made €1.89 ($2.15) per share, beating analyst estimates of €1.68 per share.

Sales from SAP's web cloud products jumped 24% from last year to €6.28 billion. This was driven by large companies moving their key files off old office computers and onto SAP's online cloud storage.

SAP Has Built Smart Automation Tools into Its Software

SAP stock dropped 40% after fear arose that cheap artificial intelligence tools would replace SAP's software. Due to these fears SAP stock dropped 40%. Highlighting the panic across Wall Street, Jefferies equity trader Jeffrey Favuzza called the software selloff the "SaaSpocalypse," adding that "trading is very much 'get me out' style selling."

SAP's management fought back by building smart automation tools right into SAP's software. Out of the 50 biggest deals SAP signed this quarter, over 90% included an artificial intelligence feature. Chief Financial Officer Dominik Asam noted, "We firmly believe that our customers do see SAP as a key contributor to their own AI stories."

Future Sales Are Looking Strong for SAP

Future sales look strong for SAP over the next twelve months. SAP's cloud backlog, a number that tracks guaranteed future sales from signed deals, grew 26% to €22.9 billion. This beat analyst expectations of 24%.

SAP's older software support sales dropped 7% as clients switched over to SAP web tools. Even so, fast web growth kept SAP moving forward. SAP kept its full-year targets for web sales and cash flows intact, though management slightly lowered profit goals to cover the costs of buying data platform Dremio and artificial intelligence firm Prior Labs.

Is SAP a Good Buy Now?

According to TipRanks, SAP stock (SAP) has a consensus Strong Buy rating among five Wall Street analysts. This rating is based on a unanimous 5 Buy ratings assigned in the past three months. The average 12-month SAP price target of $212.33 implies 45% upside from current levels. (See SAP stock forecast)
data.author: Annika Masrani
